title: HRC Steel Price Rises to 1075.00 USD/T, Highest Since January 2024
summary: Hot‑Rolled Coil (HRC) steel prices increased to 1,075.00 USD per ton, the highest level since January 2024, reflecting tightening supply and resilient demand. Over the past four weeks, HRC steel has gained about 4.99%, and analysts expect prices to rise further to around 1,120.96 USD/T by the end of the quarter.

title: Steel Prices Rise to 3,195 CNY/T on Strong Seasonal Demand
summary: Steel prices in China rose to 3,195 CNY per ton, up 1.59% from the previous day, supported by strong seasonal demand and restocking by end users. Rebar futures have climbed above 3,150 CNY per ton, nearing their highest levels since August of the previous year, amid expectations of higher global steel demand.

title: US Midwest Domestic Hot‑Rolled Coil Steel Futures Prices Edge Higher
summary: US Midwest domestic hot‑rolled coil steel futures have stabilized around 1,042–1,045 USD per ton in recent trading sessions, reflecting modest upward pressure in the domestic market. The data show relatively stable prices over the last few days, indicating a consolidating trend ahead of new demand signals.

title: Steel Market Update: Flat‑Rolled Steel Prices and Trends
summary: Steel Market Update reports that flat‑rolled steel prices are firming on improving order books and tighter inventories, with lead times extending modestly. The publication highlights rising sentiment among service centers and mills as restocking activity picks up in key industrial sectors.

title: Steel Prices, News and Analysis from Global Transactions
summary: SteelOrbis provides updated steel price assessments and market commentary, noting that global steel prices are underpinned by steady demand from construction and infrastructure projects. Regional price differentials remain elevated, reflecting logistics constraints and varying supply‑demand balances.

title: Steel Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
summary: S&P Global reports that steel spot prices are trending higher across several regions, driven by restocking and expectations of improved demand in the second half of the year. The firm highlights that real‑time data and forward‑looking indicators point to a gradual tightening of the global steel market.

title: United States Steel Corporation Announces $1.9 Billion Investment in New Direct Reduced Iron Facility
summary: United States Steel Corporation announced a $1.9 billion investment to construct a new direct reduced iron (DRI) facility at Big River Steel, signaling a major expansion of low‑carbon steelmaking capacity in the United States. The project is expected to enhance domestic raw material supply and support higher utilization of electric‑arc furnace steel production.

title: Steel Production in the United States Increases to 7,200 Thousand Tonnes in March 2026
summary: U.S. steel production rose to 7,200 thousand tonnes in March 2026, up from 6,500 thousand tonnes in February, reflecting stronger output and improving capacity utilization. The data show that U.S. steel production remains above the long‑run average despite ongoing global market volatility.

title: Domestic Raw Steel Production and Capacity Utilization for Week Ending April 25, 2026
summary: In the week ending April 25, 2026, U.S. domestic raw steel production reached 1,830,000 net tons, with a capability utilization rate of 79.3 percent. Regional breakdowns show strong output in the Southern and Great Lakes districts, indicating robust demand and stable operating rates across major steel‑producing regions.

title: ArcelorMittal Advances European Steel Sustainability with New Continuous Annealing Line in Belgium
summary: ArcelorMittal has commissioned a new continuous annealing line in Belgium as part of its European steel sustainability initiatives. This advancement represents progress in modernizing steel production processes to reduce environmental impact.

title: Copper futures steadied after five consecutive sessions of losses
summary: Copper futures stabilized around $5.9 per pound on Thursday after a five-day losing streak, supported by stronger-than-expected manufacturing expansion in China. Industrial metals remained under pressure from the Middle East conflict, though the Chinese economic data provided some relief to the market.

title: Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean mining company Codelco announced that its El Teniente smelter is set to restart on Thursday following a collapse. This development could impact global copper supply dynamics.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Reports 71,417 Tonnes of Copper in Anode Produced by Kamoa-Kakula in Q1 2026
summary: Ivanhoe Mines' on-site smelter ramped up to 60% capacity, producing 71,417 tonnes of copper anode in Q1 2026. The company's Platreef mine is advancing on schedule with commercial production expected mid-year, and construction of Shaft #3 is complete. Revised guidance projects copper production to exceed 500,000 tonnes per annum from 2028 onwards.

title: Capstone Copper Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
summary: Capstone Copper produced 47,960 tonnes of contained copper in Q1 2026 at C1 cash costs of $2.66/lb. The company achieved record adjusted EBITDA over the last six quarters and record adjusted earnings per share.

title: Copper Market News & Forecasts
summary: Ivanhoe Mines' Kamoa-Kakula copper concentrate production fell to 61,906 tonnes in Q1 2026, down 54% year-over-year, as the company evaluates local third-party concentrate purchases to advance smelter ramp-up. Global sulfuric acid shortages are impacting copper production capacity.
